summary: Machine policy for lock-screen background motion. The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file documents the same Personalization policy key and AnimateLockScreenBackground value used by the app.

setting.name: Lock Screen Background Motion
setting.casual_explanation: Some devices can show a subtle motion effect on the lock-screen background. This policy decides whether that motion is prevented.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable lock-screen motion to make the lock screen feel calmer, more static, or more predictable on supported devices.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling motion reduces visual movement, but it also removes a subtle visual effect some people like.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents AnimateLockScreenBackground through ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay and local policy files map it to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\AnimateLockScreenBackground. The public documentation clearly explains the enabled policy behavior and the baseline behavior when disabled, but it does not publish a raw DWORD table, so this record models the documented enforced state plus the default unset state.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force a lock-screen motion policy and the default visual behavior remains in place.

allowed_values.label: Prevent lock-screen background motion
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and the lock-screen background stays static instead of using the subtle motion effect.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AnimateLockScreenBackground = 1, which matches the documented enabled policy state for preventing motion.

windows_defaults.label: Windows machine-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 client devices with accelerometer-backed lock-screen motion support

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Microsoft help text describes motion as available when the setting is not configured and the device has the required hardware support.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want the default lock-screen effect
avoid_for: Users who prefer a fully static lock screen

states.rationale: Leave lock-screen motion under default Windows behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to preserve the standard lock-screen feel.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to remove subtle motion from the lock screen.
tradeoffs: Default lock-screen visual behavior is preserved.
Some supported devices may still show subtle motion.

recommended_profiles.label: Static lock screen
avoid_for: Users who like the subtle motion effect

states.rationale: Prevent the lock-screen background motion effect.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the lock screen to stay visually static.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer the animated effect on supported devices.
tradeoffs: The lock screen feels calmer and more static.
The motion effect is removed on supported devices.

evidence.title: Microsoft ADMX_ControlPanelDisplay Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the lock-screen personalization policies on this page, including AnimateLockScreenBackground and the baseline behavior when the policy is disabled or not configured.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the machine policy to HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\AnimateLockScreenBackground.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy prevents lock-screen background motion and shows the traditional static background image, while motion can occur on supported devices when the setting is not configured.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AnimateLockScreenBackground = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft ControlPanelDisplay.admx file explicitly defines AnimateLockScreenBackground under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization, and the paired ADML help text documents that enabling the policy prevents lock-screen background motion. The app writes that exact enabled state.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft ADMX file names the same machine policy surface used by the app. The paired ADML help text states that enabling the setting prevents lock-screen motion and shows the traditional static background image.
